node.title: Uber Interview Question - Sr Software Engineer - 27th March

topic.title: Uber Interview Question - Sr Software Engineer - 27th March

post.content: Given a 2d grid composed of robots, empties, and blocks, you will receive a query like [a, b, c, d], which means the right distance, left distance, top distance, and bottom distance to the nearest blocker. find the robot satisfying the query.

So what I understood from the problem is: we need to find cells where the given thresholds are matching.

we can solve with two approaches - 
Assumption (M - Rows, N - Cols)
1. **Runtime O(Mx(N^2))** - Capture all the robots and then iterate one by one on each robot and see if there is no block in the path till the threshold distance. So for each robot, we need to check it's left, right, bottom and up path to confirm that there is no block.

2. **Runtime O(MxN), Space O(MxN)** - In this approach instead of picking robots one by one, we try to build a temporary matrix where each cell has 4 distance-values (p, q, r, s) where p represents the nearest blocker in the left side, q represents the nearest blocker to the right side and so on.
Now once we build this matrix, we can iterate over the original matrix and pick the cells which have robots, and we can find the distance to the nearest blocker using the temporary matrix that we just created. If all 4 bounds are matching, then we add the current cell to the answer otherwise we move to the next cell until we iterate over all the cells of the original matrix. 

Hope this helps.
Was it asked in phone screening or onsite, which location?
it was asked in first round of interview for SDE-2
can you explain more didnt understand the question
